---
title: "Guilford Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Spaces of Functions and Sets", "Sher, R. B.", "uncategorized", "59", "Sher, R. B.", "The underlying theme of this guide is that structures (topological in this case) on sets can lead to structures on certain associated sets (e.g. sets of functions, subsets, etc.), that this can often be done in many ways, and that what results is sometimes of great interest. For those who have not been introduced to basic topology, or whose preparation has been inadequate, an introduction to general topology is included. This guide could be used as supplementary reading for a class, as a directed study project, as a guide for an undergraduate research project, or in a seminar. In addition, it is suitable as the basis for an introductory topology course taught along the lines of the Moore method.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./spaces-of-functions-and-sets_sher.pdf)

This course guide was originally published by the Guilford College Journal of Undergraduate Mathematics as a volume in the Monographs on Undergraduate Mathematics series.

This course guide may be reproduced and distributed if the following conditions are met:

- State that it is the property of the Math Department of Guilford College, and is protected under copyright by Guilford.
- Take no profit from its distribution. (You may recover copying costs if you are using these for a class.)
